title: galium serpenticum subsp. wenatchicum
intro: Galium serpenticum subsp. wenatchicum, also known as Wenatchee Bedstraw, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the Wenatchee Mountains in Washington, USA. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Wenatchee Bedstraw include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
